summary: I reviewed the "Human in the Loop Pause" diff — the new pause/menu/restart services (`Pause`, `RerunMenuStep`, `RestartDefine`), the `ManagerTick` pause/restart handling, the `Convergence` query, the `Approve`/`AnswerQuestions` status widenings, the define panel view, controller/route additions, and the migration — reading the surrounding models (`Step`, `StepRun`, `Phase`), the worker claim query, and the Define seed template to trace real execution paths. The architecture is sound (thin controllers, POROs with `.call`→`Result`, artifact-identity step resolution, merge-gated convergence), and paused single-step re-runs correctly execute since worker claiming isn't phase-gated. I landed on **needs_work** with three findings: a **major** correctness bug where "Repeat from the Beginning" silently skips any step whose iteration was individually advanced by a prior menu re-run or Ask-Human answer (because `RestartDefine` leans on `ManagerTick`'s `current_iteration < target_iteration` dispatch gate), plus two **minor** issues — geometric duplication of human feedback across repeated restarts, and `Pause` lacking a Define-phase guard so a crafted POST can strand any running phase. The verdict is written to `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/code-quality-critic/verdict.json`.
